content: I'm setting up my home office after returning from my sabbatical in Eastern Europe. I need a setup that works for both my interior design work and my hobbies like woodworking and home brewing.
Welcome back! What kind of work do you do in interior design - is it mostly 2D drawings or do you use 3D modeling software?

I use SketchUp and Revit mostly for commercial spaces. I just finished a big hotel lobby redesign. The travel really inspired me - I visited 12 countries and took thousands of photos of architectural details.
That sounds amazing. For your setup, you'll want a good monitor for detailed design work. Have you thought about dual monitors or a single ultrawide?

I'm leaning toward dual monitors so I can have my design software on one and reference photos on the other. I'm 46 now and my eyes aren't what they used to be - I need a bigger display than my old 24-inch.
Two 27-inch monitors would give you plenty of workspace. What about your computer - are you looking for a desktop or keeping your laptop setup?

I need something powerful but quiet. I do a lot of video calls with clients, and my old desktop's fans were so loud. Plus I want to run some chess engines while I play online - I'm trying to improve my rating.
A silent PC build with good cooling could work. Have you considered an AMD Ryzen setup? They're efficient and run cooler than some Intel options.
I've heard good things about Ryzen. I also need to run some Thai cooking videos while I'm working - I learned so many recipes in Thailand and I've been trying to perfect them at home.
That's a fun multitask! For your woodworking hobby, do you use any CAD software for designing furniture or projects?

I use Fusion 360 for my woodworking designs. I'm building a new dining table inspired by a medieval design I saw in Prague. The computer needs to handle both the 3D modeling and my design software without lag.
Fusion 360 is resource-intensive. You'll want at least 32GB RAM and a solid GPU. Have you thought about storage - SSD for speed or larger HDD for your project files?

I have about 2TB of design files and photos from my travels. I was thinking a 1TB SSD for the OS and programs, plus a 4TB HDD for storage. I also want to start a travel blog about my Eastern Europe trip - I took so many notes in coffee shops across Budapest and Krakow.
That's a smart storage setup. For the blog, you might want a good microphone for voiceovers when you narrate your travel stories. Do you have a preference for Windows or Mac?

I've always used Windows for the software compatibility. My calligraphy hobby also benefits from certain Windows programs I can't get on Mac. I'm trying to digitize my handwritten recipes and travel journals.
Windows would give you more flexibility for all your hobbies. Have you considered an all-in-one PC to save desk space, or do you prefer a tower setup?

I like the idea of a tower so I can upgrade components later. I might get into more advanced brewing techniques and want to run fermentation monitoring software. My home brewery setup is getting pretty sophisticated.
A tower gives you that upgrade path. You could even add a small touchscreen for quick controls while you're cooking or brewing. What's your budget range?
I can go up to $2000 for the computer and monitors. I'm also looking at a new drawing tablet for my design work - my old one is from 2015 and the pressure sensitivity is off.
With $2000, you can get a really solid setup. Maybe a 27-inch 4K monitor for color accuracy in your design work, plus a good 1080p secondary monitor.

That sounds perfect. I want to calibrate the colors exactly - I had trouble matching fabrics to digital designs on my last project. The client wanted a specific shade of blue that looked different on every screen.
A color-calibrated monitor is essential for interior design. You might also want a good webcam for client meetings - something that shows your work area clearly when you're discussing projects.

Great idea. I'll look for a setup that lets me show my mood boards and material samples during video calls. I'm excited to get everything set up - it's been three months since I've had a proper workspace.
